question:
Suppose that $a,b,c,A,B,C$  are real numbers, $a\ne 0$ and $A \ne 0$, such that
\[
          | a x^2 + b x + c | \leq | A x^2 + B x + C |
\]
for all real numbers  $x$. Show that
\[
              | b^2 - 4 a c | \leq | B^2 - 4 A C |.
\]
solution:
We split into three cases.
Note first that $|A| \geq |a|$, by applying the condition for large $x$.

\textbf{Case 1: $B^2 - 4AC > 0$.}
In this case $Ax^2 + Bx + C$ has two distinct real roots $r_1$ and $r_2$.
The condition implies that $ax^2 + bx + c$ also vanishes at $r_1$ and $r_2$,
so $b^2 - 4ac > 0$.
Now
\begin{align*}
B^2 - 4AC &= A^2(r_1-r_2)^2 \\
&\geq a^2(r_1 - r_2)^2 \\
&= b^2 - 4ac.
\end{align*}

\textbf{Case 2: $B^2 - 4AC \leq 0$ and $b^2 - 4ac \leq 0$.}
Assume without loss of generality that $A \geq a > 0$, and that $B=0$
(by shifting $x$). Then $Ax^2 + Bx + C \geq ax^2 + bx + c \geq 0$ for all $x$;
in particular, $C \geq c \geq 0$. Thus
\begin{align*}
4AC - B^2 &= 4AC \\
&\geq 4ac \\
&\geq 4ac - b^2.
\end{align*}
Alternate derivation (due to Robin Chapman): the ellipse
$Ax^2 + Bxy + Cy^2 = 1$ is contained within
the ellipse $ax^2 + bxy + cy^2 = 1$,
and their respective enclosed areas are $\pi/(4AC-B^2)$ and
$\pi/(4ac-b^2)$.

\textbf{Case 3: $B^2 - 4AC \leq 0$ and $b^2 - 4ac > 0$.}
Since $Ax^2 + Bx + C$ has a graph not crossing the $x$-axis,
so do $(Ax^2 + Bx + C) \pm (ax^2 + bx + c)$. Thus
\begin{gather*}
(B-b)^2 - 4(A-a)(C-c) \leq 0, \\
(B+b)^2 - 4(A+a)(C+c) \leq 0
\end{gather*}
and adding these together yields
\[
2(B^2 - 4AC) + 2(b^2 - 4ac) \leq 0.
\]
Hence $b^2 - 4ac \leq 4AC - B^2$, as desired.

descriptive_long.question:
Suppose that $smallcoeffa,smallcoeffb,smallcoeffc,bigcoeffa,bigcoeffb,bigcoeffc$  are real numbers, $smallcoeffa\ne 0$ and $bigcoeffa \ne 0$, such that
\[
          | smallcoeffa\, variable^2 + smallcoeffb\, variable + smallcoeffc | \leq | bigcoeffa\, variable^2 + bigcoeffb\, variable + bigcoeffc |
\]
for all real numbers  $variable$. Show that
\[
              | smallcoeffb^2 - 4\, smallcoeffa\, smallcoeffc | \leq | bigcoeffb^2 - 4\, bigcoeffa\, bigcoeffc |.
\]

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Suppose that $sunflower,riverbank,tophatbox,mapleleaf,starlitpath,bedroomlamp$  are real numbers, $sunflower\ne 0$ and $mapleleaf \ne 0$, such that
\[
          | sunflower hummingbird^2 + riverbank hummingbird + tophatbox | \leq | mapleleaf hummingbird^2 + starlitpath hummingbird + bedroomlamp |
\]
for all real numbers  $hummingbird$. Show that
\[
              | riverbank^2 - 4 sunflower tophatbox | \leq | starlitpath^2 - 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp |.
\]
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
We split into three cases.
Note first that $|mapleleaf| \geq |sunflower|$, by applying the condition for large $hummingbird$.

\textbf{Case 1: $starlitpath^2 - 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp > 0$.}
In this case $mapleleaf hummingbird^2 + starlitpath hummingbird + bedroomlamp$ has two distinct real roots $raincloudone$ and $raincloudtwo$.
The condition implies that $sunflower hummingbird^2 + riverbank hummingbird + tophatbox$ also vanishes at $raincloudone$ and $raincloudtwo$,
so $riverbank^2 - 4 sunflower tophatbox > 0$.
Now
\begin{align*}
starlitpath^2 - 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp &= mapleleaf^2(raincloudone-raincloudtwo)^2 \\
&\geq sunflower^2(raincloudone - raincloudtwo)^2 \\
&= riverbank^2 - 4 sunflower tophatbox.
\end{align*}

\textbf{Case 2: $starlitpath^2 - 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp \leq 0$ and $riverbank^2 - 4 sunflower tophatbox \leq 0$.}
Assume without loss of generality that $mapleleaf \geq sunflower > 0$, and that $starlitpath=0$ (by shifting $hummingbird$). Then $mapleleaf hummingbird^2 + starlitpath hummingbird + bedroomlamp \geq sunflower hummingbird^2 + riverbank hummingbird + tophatbox \geq 0$ for all $hummingbird$; in particular, $bedroomlamp \geq tophatbox \geq 0$. Thus
\begin{align*}
4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp - starlitpath^2 &= 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp \\
&\geq 4 sunflower tophatbox \\
&\geq 4 sunflower tophatbox - riverbank^2.
\end{align*}
Alternate derivation (due to Robin Chapman): the ellipse
$mapleleaf hummingbird^2 + starlitpath hummingbird candlewick + bedroomlamp candlewick^2 = 1$ is contained within
the ellipse $sunflower hummingbird^2 + riverbank hummingbird candlewick + tophatbox candlewick^2 = 1$, and their respective enclosed areas are $\pi/(4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp - starlitpath^2)$ and $\pi/(4 sunflower tophatbox - riverbank^2)$.

\textbf{Case 3: $starlitpath^2 - 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp \leq 0$ and $riverbank^2 - 4 sunflower tophatbox > 0$.}
Since $mapleleaf hummingbird^2 + starlitpath hummingbird + bedroomlamp$ has a graph not crossing the $hummingbird$-axis, so do $(mapleleaf hummingbird^2 + starlitpath hummingbird + bedroomlamp) \pm (sunflower hummingbird^2 + riverbank hummingbird + tophatbox)$. Thus
\begin{gather*}
(starlitpath-riverbank)^2 - 4(mapleleaf-sunflower)(bedroomlamp-tophatbox) \leq 0, \\
(starlitpath+riverbank)^2 - 4(mapleleaf+sunflower)(bedroomlamp+tophatbox) \leq 0
\end{gather*}
and adding these together yields
\[
2(starlitpath^2 - 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp) + 2(riverbank^2 - 4 sunflower tophatbox) \leq 0.
\]
Hence $riverbank^2 - 4 sunflower tophatbox \leq 4 mapleleaf bedroomlamp - starlitpath^2$, as desired.

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Suppose that $zerocoeff,quadraticcoef,variableterm,zerocapital,quadcapital,variablecap$  are real numbers, $zerocoeff\ne 0$ and $zerocapital \ne 0$, such that
\[
          |\, zerocoeff\, constantval^{2} + quadraticcoef\, constantval + variableterm \,| \leq |\, zerocapital\, constantval^{2} + quadcapital\, constantval + variablecap \,|
\]
for all real numbers  $constantval$. Show that
\[
              |\, quadraticcoef^{2} - 4\, zerocoeff\, variableterm \,| \leq |\, quadcapital^{2} - 4\, zerocapital\, variablecap \,|.
\]
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
We split into three cases.
Note first that $|\zerocapital| \geq |\zerocoeff|$, by applying the condition for large $\constantval$.

\textbf{Case 1: $\quadcapital^{2} - 4\zerocapital\variablecap > 0$.}
In this case $\zerocapital\, constantval^{2} + \quadcapital\, constantval + \variablecap$ has two distinct real roots $\peakfirst$ and $\peaksecond$.
The condition implies that $\zerocoeff\, constantval^{2} + \quadraticcoef\, constantval + \variableterm$ also vanishes at $\peakfirst$ and $\peaksecond$, so $\quadraticcoef^{2} - 4\zerocoeff\variableterm > 0$.
Now
\begin{align*}
\quadcapital^{2} - 4\zerocapital\variablecap &= \zerocapital^{2}(\peakfirst-\peaksecond)^{2} \\
&\geq \zerocoeff^{2}(\peakfirst-\peaksecond)^{2} \\
&= \quadraticcoef^{2} - 4\zerocoeff\variableterm.
\end{align*}

\textbf{Case 2: $\quadcapital^{2} - 4\zerocapital\variablecap \leq 0$ and $\quadraticcoef^{2} - 4\zerocoeff\variableterm \leq 0$.}
Assume without loss of generality that $\zerocapital \geq \zerocoeff > 0$, and that $\quadcapital = 0$ (by shifting $\constantval$).
Then $\zerocapital\, constantval^{2} + \variablecap \geq \zerocoeff\, constantval^{2} + \variableterm \geq 0$ for all $\constantval$; in particular, $\variablecap \geq \variableterm \geq 0$. Thus
\begin{align*}
4\zerocapital\variablecap - \quadcapital^{2} &= 4\zerocapital\variablecap \\
&\geq 4\zerocoeff\variableterm \\
&\geq 4\zerocoeff\variableterm - \quadraticcoef^{2}.
\end{align*}
Alternate derivation (due to Robin Chapman): the ellipse $\zerocapital\, constantval^{2} + \quadcapital\, constantval\, fixedpoint + \variablecap\, fixedpoint^{2} = 1$ is contained within the ellipse $\zerocoeff\, constantval^{2} + \quadraticcoef\, constantval\, fixedpoint + \variableterm\, fixedpoint^{2} = 1$, and their respective enclosed areas are $\pi/(4\zerocapital\variablecap-\quadcapital^{2})$ and $\pi/(4\zerocoeff\variableterm-\quadraticcoef^{2})$.

\textbf{Case 3: $\quadcapital^{2} - 4\zerocapital\variablecap \leq 0$ and $\quadraticcoef^{2} - 4\zerocoeff\variableterm > 0$.}
Since $\zerocapital\, constantval^{2} + \quadcapital\, constantval + \variablecap$ has a graph not crossing the $\constantval$-axis, so do $(\zerocapital\, constantval^{2} + \quadcapital\, constantval + \variablecap) \pm (\zerocoeff\, constantval^{2} + \quadraticcoef\, constantval + \variableterm)$. Thus
\begin{gather*}
(\quadcapital-\quadraticcoef)^{2} - 4(\zerocapital-\zerocoeff)(\variablecap-\variableterm) \leq 0, \\
(\quadcapital+\quadraticcoef)^{2} - 4(\zerocapital+\zerocoeff)(\variablecap+\variableterm) \leq 0
\end{gather*}
and adding these together yields
\[
2(\quadcapital^{2} - 4\zerocapital\variablecap) + 2(\quadraticcoef^{2} - 4\zerocoeff\variableterm) \leq 0.
\]
Hence $\quadraticcoef^{2} - 4\zerocoeff\variableterm \leq 4\zerocapital\variablecap - \quadcapital^{2}$, as desired.

garbled_string.question:
Suppose that $zqmsnpcg,vxrldkwe,jpsqhxot,kmgnwrzb,cvfyladu,nbxqterh$  are real numbers, $zqmsnpcg\ne 0$ and $kmgnwrzb \ne 0$, such that
\[
          | zqmsnpcg qzxwvtnp^2 + vxrldkwe qzxwvtnp + jpsqhxot | \leq | kmgnwrzb qzxwvtnp^2 + cvfyladu qzxwvtnp + nbxqterh |
\]
for all real numbers  $qzxwvtnp$. Show that
\[
              | vxrldkwe^2 - 4 zqmsnpcg jpsqhxot | \leq | cvfyladu^2 - 4 kmgnwrzb nbxqterh |.
\]

kernel_variant.question:
Let u, v, w, U, V, W be real numbers with u \neq  0 and U \neq  0.  Assume that
          |u t^{2}+v t+w| \leq  |U t^{2}+V t+W|   holds for every real t.
Prove that
            |v^{2}-4u w| \leq  |V^{2}-4U W|.
kernel_variant.solution:
Put
    p(t)=u t^{2}+v t+w ,   q(t)=U t^{2}+V t+W  \;(t\in \mathbb{R}),
and denote their discriminants by
    \Delta _p = v^{2}-4u w,   \Delta _q = V^{2}-4U W.
The hypothesis is
                     |p(t)| \leq  |q(t)|  for every t.              (H)

A preliminary observation
-------------------------
For |t|\to \infty  the quadratic terms dominate, so from (H)
        |u| |t|^{2} - |q(t)| \leq  |p(t)| \leq  |q(t)|
we obtain |u| \leq  |U|.                                            (1)

We distinguish three cases according to the sign of \Delta _q.

Case 1 :  \Delta _q > 0
-----------------
Then q has two distinct real zeros r_1 \neq  r_2.  At those points |p(r_i)| \leq  |q(r_i)| = 0,
so p(r_i) = 0 and therefore \Delta _p > 0.  Hence p and q possess the same roots; writing
      q(t) = U (t-r_1)(t-r_2),   p(t) = u (t-r_1)(t-r_2),
we get \Delta _q = U^2(r_1-r_2)^2 and \Delta _p = u^2(r_1-r_2)^2.  By (1), |u| \leq  |U|, thus
            |\Delta _p| = u^2(r_1-r_2)^2 \leq  U^2(r_1-r_2)^2 = |\Delta _q|.

Case 2 :  \Delta _q \leq  0  and  \Delta _p \leq  0
-------------------------------
Multiply p and q by -1 if necessary so that U>0; then q(t) > 0 for all t.

Step 1 - eliminate the linear term of q.
Choose h = V/(2U) and set t = y - h.  Define
      p*(y)=p(y-h)=u y^{2}+v' y+w',
      q*(y)=q(y-h)=U y^{2}+C,
with C = (4U W - V^{2})/(4U) \geq  0 (because \Delta _q \leq  0).  Inequality (H) becomes
                          |p*(y)| \leq  q*(y)  (y\in \mathbb{R})               (H*)
and \Delta _{p*} = \Delta _p.

Step 2 - two immediate bounds.
Evaluating (H*) at y = 0 gives |w'| \leq  C, while (1) still yields |u| \leq  U.

Step 3 - a sign restriction on u w'.
Since \Delta _p = v'^{2} - 4u w' \leq  0, we have 4u w' \geq  v'^{2} \geq  0, hence
                       u w' \geq  0.                              (2)
Consequently |u w'| = u w'.

Step 4 - the desired inequality.
Because v'^{2} \geq  0 and \Delta _p \leq  0,
      |\Delta _p| = -\Delta _p = 4u w' - v'^{2} \leq  4u w' = 4|u w'| \leq  4|u| |w'|
              \leq  4|u| C \leq  4U C = -\Delta _q = |\Delta _q|.
Therefore |\Delta _p| \leq  |\Delta _q|.

Case 3 :  \Delta _q \leq  0  and  \Delta _p > 0
--------------------------------
Again choose the overall sign so that U>0; hence q(t) > 0 for every t.  For any real t,
                q(t) \pm  p(t) \geq  |q(t)| - |p(t)| \geq  0,
so the quadratics r_\pm (t)=q(t)\pm p(t) have no real roots and their discriminants satisfy
        (V\pm v)^{2} - 4(U\pm u)(W\pm w) \leq  0.                          (3)
Adding the two inequalities in (3) gives
        2(V^{2}+v^{2}) \leq  4[(U+u)(W+w)+(U-u)(W-w)]
                       = 8 U W + 8 u w,                      (4)
whence
        V^{2}+v^{2} \leq  4 U W + 4 u w.                          (5)

Now compute
 |\Delta _q| - |\Delta _p| = (4U W - V^{2}) - (v^{2} - 4u w)
                = 4(U W + u w) - (V^{2}+v^{2}).
The right-hand side is non-negative by (5), therefore |\Delta _p| \leq  |\Delta _q|.

Conclusion
----------
In all three cases we have proved
                |v^{2} - 4u w| \leq  |V^{2} - 4U W|.
Thus the statement is established for every choice of real numbers u,v,w,U,V,W with u\neq 0, U\neq 0 that satisfy (H).

core_steps: Compare leading coefficients via the |p(x)|Ōēż|q(x)| condition for large |x|, yielding |A|Ōēź|a|.
Split into cases according to the sign of the larger discriminant B┬▓ŌłÆ4AC.
If B┬▓ŌłÆ4AC>0, shared real roots force b┬▓ŌłÆ4ac>0 and |A|Ōēź|a| gives a┬▓(rŌéüŌłÆrŌéé)┬▓ŌēżA┬▓(rŌéüŌłÆrŌéé)┬▓.
If both discriminantsŌēż0, translate x to kill the linear term and compare constant terms (or enclosedŌĆÉarea of nested ellipses).
If signs differ, use non-negativity of (q┬▒p)(x) to bound the sums of discriminants, then isolate |b┬▓ŌłÆ4ac|.

slot1.description: Names chosen for the six coefficients and the variable; any renaming leaves the argument intact.

slot2.description: Numbering / order of the three case analyses; they can be treated in any sequence.
